headline: Managing Partner at SoftBank Investment Advisers
industry: Mandanti di venture capital e private equity
summary: Brett B. Rochkind is the Managing Partner of BRK Capital, a global growth investing platform based in Silicon Valley. BRK is inspired by Warren Buffett, Chairman & CEO of Berkshire Hathaway (BRK), and the values of Being (living and appreciating the human experience in all aspects), Rock (serving as a solid rock of support to family, friends, partners, entrepreneurs and the community) and Kind (being a loving, friendly, generous, and benevolent person). The main objective of BRK is to compound capital at 19.9% for 60 years as Mr. Buffett has done for BRK shareholders from 1964 through 2024.  Brett is a Managing Partner at the SoftBank Vision Fund, the largest global technology investing platform with over $150 billion in assets across over 500 portfolio companies. Prior to SoftBank, Brett was a Managing Director at General Atlantic (GA), a global investment firm where he led GA’s Silicon Valley office and was the Global Co-Head of the Technology sector. Brett was at GA for 15 years beginning in New York in 2002 investing in Software, Services and Internet businesses and helped GA expand its investment operations into Brazil and Latin America. In 2009, Brett moved to Hong Kong to help expand GA’s China presence and explore opportunities across China, India and Asia. In 2017, Brett left GA to build a growth fund with Charles River Ventures (CRV), one of the first venture capital firms founded in 1970.  Brett has invested in leading companies globally including Alibaba (BABA), Airbnb (ABNB), AlphaSense, Altair Engineering (ALTR), AppDynamics (Cisco acq.), Appirio (Wipro acq.), Box (BOX), Cato Networks, Databricks, IHS Markit (S&P acq.), Lenovo (0992), Linx (Stone acq.), Liquid Death, Meituan (3690), MercadoLibre (MELI), Navan, Nubank (NU), Philz Coffee, Perplexity, Remote, Runway, Slice, SSA Global (Infor acq.), Tines, Tipalti, Uber (UBER) and Wiz.  Brett serves on the New York University Board of Trustees, NYU Stern School of Business Executive Board, and the Tipping Point Community’s Leadership Council, which works to create opportunities for everyone to prosper in the San Francisco Bay Area.  Brett is married to an amazing wife, Rivi, and together they are raising 3 incredible Kids. He is a proud 5.00 star Uber driver, but an inept Philz barista. Brett is also a terrible golfer particularly for a former golf caddy and collegiate golfer, loves playing mediocre tennis, supports most of the Washington, DC area sports teams (especially the Baltimore Orioles), and has adopted the SF Bay Area sports teams, particularly whichever one is winning.
organization_description_1: At SoftBank Investment Advisers, we believe the AI Revolution is underway. Through the SoftBank Vision Funds, we are investing more than $140 billion in the businesses and technologies that will help make it possible. We offer the operational expertise, global network, and patient capital required to help exceptional founders build market-leading companies.
education_1: New York University - Leonard N. Stern School of Business
cs_subject: what's missing in cash yield today?
cs_body: Brett, we've been connected for a while and I wanted to reach out. I'm advising a small, experienced team exploring what operators actually need from a yield product for idle cash.   Could I steal 15 minutes for your candid view? Happy to share anonymized patterns from others in the space. Greg
